02 ◆ What I Deliver

The Problems I Solve

Walking into the most complex, high-stakes technology environments and finding a clear, honest, pragmatic path forward. Technology-agnostic by philosophy. Multi-domain by experience. AI-fluent by daily practice.

⛤ EA
Enterprise Architecture at Scale

Governing portfolios of 1,200+ vendors and 2,000+ systems. Translating the most intricate architectures into executive-grade narratives that move decisions forward.

◆ AI
AI Strategy & Governance

Authoring enterprise AI/LLM adoption roadmaps and Responsible AI frameworks. Claude, Gemini Pro, GPT, Cursor, Dataiku, and NotebookLM are active daily workflow tools.

◆ M&A
M&A Integration Architecture

Led technology architecture for Lincoln Financial’s $3.3B Liberty Life acquisition ◆ 200+ systems, 1,200 components, 2+ petabytes migrated to AWS across 34 technology sectors.

△ Cloud
Cloud Transformation

Assessed 2,000+ applications for AWS cloud migration readiness. BCDR consolidation from 12 locations to 6. Multi-year TCO optimization roadmaps delivering $39M+ annual savings.

○ DT
Digital Transformation

From monolithic legacy ecosystems to modern, cloud-native, API-driven architectures ◆ on schedule, without torching organizational relationships. The goal: Enterprise Eudaimonia.

■ Data
Data & Visualization Strategy

“Perhaps the most skilled data visualizer I have encountered in my career.” Leadership is storytelling. The best architecture fails if the boardroom can’t understand it.

03 ◆ Validated by Industry Leadership

What People Say

Endorsements spanning 16 years ◆ from C-suite clients and chief architects to data scientists and engineering leads.

// Why These Voices Matter
Matthew Daniels
VP, Chief Architect at Lincoln Financial (Fortune 50). Jesse’s direct executive client for 6+ years. He personally oversaw the $3.3B M&A program and signed off on every major architecture decision in the portfolio.
► The highest-stakes endorsement possible ◆ the C-suite principal with direct financial accountability for the outcomes Jesse delivered.
Bernard Ong
Principal Data Scientist and Kaggle Competition winner. Data scientists are analytically rigorous ◆ they do not give praise easily. His endorsement validates Jesse’s visualization claims from the most credible quantitative lens available.
► Hardest audience to impress. Peer validation from the most skeptical technical discipline.
Dan Hughes
Founder of Wittij Consulting and published author of the definitive TOGAF practitioner guide. His methodology was recognized by The Open Group in 2022. Jesse was employee #3 ◆ directly chosen by the man who literally wrote the book on EA.
► Peer-to-peer validation from the canonical author of the EA field’s defining text.
J. DiPadua
Multi-engagement technology leader through complex modernization programs. His “politically survivable” phrasing names the hardest and most underrated EA skill: delivering technically correct outcomes while keeping the organization intact.
► Names the rarest combo. Most architects are technically correct or politically viable. Rarely both.
Peer Architects
Architecture peers at Lincoln Financial who work in the same discipline and review the same artifacts daily. Peer respect is rarer than client praise in this profession ◆ peers know exactly what the work actually involved.
► “Most skilled data visualizer I’ve encountered” ◆ from someone who encounters architects every day.

05 ◆ Professional History

Career Timeline

Wittij Consulting / Lincoln Financial Group
Enterprise Architect Advisor to Chief Architect ◆ AI Strategist
Oct 2018 ◆ Present ◆ Fortune 150 Embedded ◆ New England, USA

Embedded at Lincoln Financial Group (Fortune 150) reporting directly to the Chief Architect. Portfolio: 1,200+ vendors, 2,000+ systems, multi-petabyte data across 12+ locations. Advisor to ARB — every major platform, vendor, and integration decision passed through this body., SRB, FAM, Cloud Migration, EA Intake, and Peer Review committees. Solution architected 70+ enterprise programs end-to-end. Led technology architecture for the $3.3B Liberty Life acquisition: 200+ systems, 1,200 components, 2+ petabytes to AWS across 34 technology sectors. BCDR consolidated from 12 to 6 locations. System interfaces reduced 44%. Described as “indispensable” by VP Chief Architect. Delivered via Wittij Consulting, led by Dan Hughes (author of the definitive TOGAF practitioner guide).

Consolidated Contact Center onto Salesforce Service Cloud across 12+ Lines of Business. Office of the CTO Channel Domain Lead governing all communications across 50M+ members.

// Terms in this section
◆ TOGAF 9 Master
The Open Group Architecture Framework. Industry’s most widely adopted EA methodology. “Master” is the highest certification level.
► Governs how Fortune 500 technology decisions get made.
◆ SAFe Agile
Scaled Agile Framework. Applies agile principles across many enterprise teams simultaneously.
► 100+ features per PI = 1 delivered per business day.
◆ ARB — every major platform, vendor, and integration decision passed through this body. / SRB
Architecture / Solution Review Board. Governance bodies that approve significant technology decisions before execution.
► Where architecture influence translates directly to enterprise risk avoidance.
◆ BCDR
Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery. Plans ensuring the enterprise survives outages or disasters.
► Consolidated 12 data centers to 6 without service disruption.
◆ M&A Architecture
Technology integration plan when two companies merge ◆ systems, data, vendors, and people combined cohesively.
► $3.3B Liberty Life: 200+ systems, 2+ petabytes, AWS.
◆ LOB
Line of Business ◆ a distinct business unit (e.g. Life Insurance, Annuities, Retirement Plans).

Why Architecture Creates Outsized Returns

Enterprise architects operate as economic architects. A single governance decision affecting 300+ programs, 2,000+ systems, or a $3.3B acquisition has compounding returns that dwarf implementation costs. The 38x ROI is not theoretical ◆ it reflects documented program outcomes across regulated industries.

$795M+ documented ÷ ~$21M estimated EA investment = 38x

// Terms in this section
◆ LLM
Large Language Model. AI trained on massive text datasets. Foundation of Claude, GPT, Gemini.
► Jesse operationalizes multiple LLMs daily in real architecture workflows.
◆ RAG
Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Gives LLMs access to private enterprise data without retraining.
► Critical for safe, auditable enterprise AI adoption at scale.
◆ Responsible AI
Frameworks governing AI fairness, transparency, accountability, and risk management. Required in financial services by regulation.
► Jesse is authoring this framework at LFG across 12+ LOBs.
◆ Agentic AI
AI systems that autonomously take actions and complete multi-step tasks with minimal human input.
► The frontier where EA governance becomes existentially critical.
◆ Enterprise LLM Adoption
The organizational, governance, and architecture work required to deploy AI tools safely at Fortune-scale.
► Most enterprises are 2–3 years behind where they think they are.

Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers
IEEE is the world's largest technical professional organization. Validates expertise at the intersection of engineering rigor and enterprise digital transformation ◆ recognized globally across 160+ countries.
◆
SEI CPAA ↗
Carnegie Mellon SEI Architecture Analysis
Carnegie Mellon's Architecture Tradeoff Analysis Method (ATAM) is the gold standard for evaluating whether architectures can meet quality requirements under real production constraints.
◆
Wittij PCA ↗
People Centric Architecture ◆ OpenGroup Recognized 2022
Formally recognized by The Open Group in 2022. Architecture designed around human behavior and organizational dynamics first ◆ the missing dimension in most enterprise frameworks.

Scaled Agile Framework
Most widely adopted enterprise agile framework ◆ 20,000+ companies worldwide. Applied to deliver 100+ features per Program Increment at Aetna. One production feature delivered every business day.

Started building automated testing systems at AOL during college. Left when real-world execution outpaced academic pace ◆ the architecture foundation was built by doing, not studying. No regrets.

12 ◆ Rethinking the Blueprint

The Transformation Blueprint

The contrast between traditional ‘Ivory Tower’ EA and the approach that actually delivers organizational flourishing.

Traditional ‘Ivory Tower’ EAJesse’s Approach
✕Governance: Manual Audits & Heavy Checklists.✓Governance: Compliance-as-a-Service ◆ metrics embedded in infra/app layers.
✕Design: DevOps. Deploy faster.✓Design: DesignOps & Zero UI ◆ build for extreme usability and human engagement.
✕Culture: Siloed, Time-Based, Reactive.✓Culture: Fluid, Task-Based, Fail-Fast Dexterity.
✕Ultimate Goal: System Uptime & Cost Reduction.✓Ultimate Goal: Enterprise Eudaimonia ◆ organizational flourishing through purpose-driven technology.
13 ◆ Methodological Framework

Enterprise Architecture Archaeology

Enterprises have a veneer of modernity underpinned by decades-old monolithic systems. Peeling back those strata ◆ uncovering the real current state before transforming it ◆ separates architects from diagram-drawers.

“You can’t really know where you are going until you know where you have been.” Seventy percent of all architecture is done in the library, sifting through 20,000+ artifacts to find the truth.

Surface: Modern UI / Web Apps
The visible layer ◆ often misleadingly modern.
Crust: APIs / Middleware / CMDBs
Integration and orchestration ◆ where technical debt hides in plain sight.
Bedrock: Mainframes / Tribal Knowledge / Legacy Data
The real load-bearing walls of the enterprise. Often undocumented.
“The end result was ‘indispensable’ to the enterprise. The work required a significant amount of digging.” ◆ EA Archaeology in action, $3.3B M&A.
Fig: An architect descends into a new LOB ◆ jessemyer.com/perspective
Fig: Zoomable UI Architecture ◆ making complex systems navigable at every leadership level
// Terms in this section
◆ CMDB
Configuration Management Database. Registry of all IT assets and their dependencies.
► Often incomplete. Archaeology uncovers what the CMDB missed.
◆ Technical Debt
Accumulated cost of shortcuts in past development. Accrues interest ◆ slowing future work exponentially.
► 70% of enterprise transformation work is debt archaeology.
◆ Tribal Knowledge
Critical system knowledge that exists only in people’s heads. When they leave, it’s lost permanently.
► The most dangerous form of debt in aging enterprises.
◆ Current / Future State
Foundational EA deliverable: rigorously documenting where you are before designing where you’re going.
► You cannot transform what you haven’t accurately mapped.

Life in Motion

Macro
Crystal formations. Rivet patterns. Engine details. The geometry hidden in plain sight ◆ at 1:1 magnification.

Motion

19 ◆ High Performance, High Precision

Beyond the Architecture

The same rigor and precision applied to enterprise systems carries over. Risk assessment at 120mph. System monitoring at altitude. Framing a perfect shot under pressure. These are not separate from the work ◆ they are the same skills, different domain.

► Active Track Driver
MyersMiles Racing & Media

36 documented events at Lime Rock, COTA, Watkins Glen, Virginia International, Pocono. Savanna Myer, wife, co-owner & lead photographer shoots every event. SCDA ◆ COM Sports Car Club ◆ EdgeAddicts + Otto Racing.

► FAA Student Pilot
Aviation

Mastering strict protocols, system monitoring, and complex navigational environments. Halfway to license. Expanding MyersMiles into aerial photography. Every preflight is an architecture review.

► Co-Owner, MyersMiles
Photography & Visual Media

The rigorous artistic eye applied to motorsports translates directly to enterprise data visualization. Savanna Myer, wife & lead photographer, is the visual talent defining the brand.

► Global Travelogue
Travel & Perspective

Edinburgh during Storm Deidre at the Waldorf Astoria. France for a honeymoon. Ireland for the Guinness. Seeing the world whole ◆ not through a single lens ◆ applies equally to enterprise transformation strategy.

20 ◆ Background & Credentials

The Background

Army-brat background ◆ Fort Knox, then Fairbanks Alaska. First computer was a Microprose flight sim on my father’s military detachment hardware. Taught myself HTML on Geocities. Started at AOL during college building automated testing systems. Dropped out of Ohio State CS because the real-world work was more interesting.

Settled in Connecticut with Savanna. Co-founded MyersMiles. Halfway to an FAA pilot certificate. Big fan of Buckeye Football, scotch, red wine, and world-class kitchens.

In January 2025 I was diagnosed with Stage 3 Esophageal Cancer ◆ specifically Barrett’s Esophagus-related adenocarcinoma. Five rounds of FLOT chemotherapy. A major esophagectomy ◆ reconstructive surgery removing and rebuilding the esophagus. I’m still here ◆ with sharper focus, deeper gratitude, and a stronger commitment to doing work that matters. You remember the good people, more than the projects.

Home Command Post ◆ December 2025
